title: cornus macrophylla f. variegata
intro: Cornus macrophylla f. variegata, also known as the variegated flowering dogwood, is a plant species that belongs to the Cornaceae family and is native to East Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cornus macrophylla f. variegata include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol levels, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable herb for cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Cornus macrophylla f. variegata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, capsules, or topical creams.
